A special form of secured loan where the purpose of the loan must be specified to the lender, to purchase assets that must be fixed (not movable) property, such as a house or piece of farm land. The assets are registered as the legal property of the borrower but the lender can seize them and dispose of them if they are not satisfied with the manner in which the repayment of the loan is conducted by the borrower. Once the loan is fully repaid, the lender loses this right of seizure and the assets are then deemed to be unencumbered.

Any raised flat surface that can be walked on: a balcony; a porch; a raised patio; a flat rooftop.
An object placed against or under another, to support it; anything that supports.
The act of displacing, or the state of being displaced.
A (usually rigid), flat, rectangular measuring or drawing device with graduations in units of measurement; a straightedge with markings.
Sharpen again
To spin, turn, or revolve.
Comfortable; at ease.
To shade, to color.
A physical object whose shape is used as a guide to make other objects.
A gentle bend, such as in a road.
A person or device that cuts (in various senses).
A work, such as a blanket, composed of many different colors and shapes, sewn together to make an interesting whole.
One blade on a pair of scissors.
For a special purpose, person, or occasion.
To purify; reduce to a fine, unmixed, or pure state; to free from impurities.
Of or pertaining to calligraphy.
To lay (for example, flooring or railroad track) again.
In a miraculous manner.
To float in the air.
An image, symbol, picture, or other representation usually as an object of religious devotion.
To be in the habit of choosing something rather than something else; to favor; to like better.
A bird, usually black, of the genus Corvus, having a strong conical beak, with projecting bristles; it has a harsh, croaking call.
